<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/postgres/02-isolation/lost-update-read-committed.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

*Two app servers process a +10 deposit each: read the balance, add 10 in code, write it back.*

```
A> BEGIN;
BEGIN

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)

B> BEGIN;
BEGIN

B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)

A> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 110 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE 1

A> COMMIT;
COMMIT
```

*B computed 100 + 10 from its stale read. Nothing stops the write — A's transaction is long gone.*

```
B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 110 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE 1

B> COMMIT;
COMMIT

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     110 
(1 row)
```

*A's deposit vanished without any error. Fixes: atomic UPDATE, SELECT FOR UPDATE, or REPEATABLE READ — see the lesson.*

<small>Verified against PostgreSQL 18.4 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/database-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/postgres/02-isolation/lost-update-read-committed.ts)</small>
